description: A morning can contain a stretch of reading; 'sui kelua' places the event inside that span. The same word works with a ceremony or a counted duration. Context decides whether the event fills the span or occurs somewhere inside it. 'mua' (LOC) locates an event at a time, while 'sui' keeps temporal extent in view.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade near the alveolar ridge and send unvoiced air through its central groove for 's', then draw the tongue back and round the lips for high-back 'u'. At the syllable boundary, unround the lips and move the tongue forward into high-front 'i'. Stress 'su'.
usage_notes: The object of 'sui' has duration. With a counted span such as 'sui wi philo', English commonly uses 'for two days'.
